objective: Architectural sign-off on extracting grove-core library crate and rewiring the CLI to consume it via grove_core::.
key_changes: Two-crate lib+binary split sound: core/ holds reusable clap-free engine; cli/ is thin clap shell. Matches stack.md posture.
AC#4 holds: cargo tree -p grove-core is clap-free; clap lives only in cli/Cargo.toml. CLI concern correctly isolated.
Dep partition clean: core carries 9 engine deps with serde derive feature (REVISION 2); cli retains clap/serde/serde_json/ignore/anyhow + grove-core path dep.
REVISION 1 applied: main.rs imports {ops,registry,fetch,ingest} excluding engine; no engine:: refs remain; avoids clippy unused_imports.
Consumer rewiring correct: mcp.rs and init.rs use grove_core::; intra-core crate:: refs target only moved modules; no visibility bumps needed.
Toolchain green: clippy -D warnings exit 0; 134 tests pass (32 cli + 17 integ + 85 core); binary still grove 0.1.11; registry counts unchanged.
Pure mechanical extraction via git mv; no engine logic rewritten; history preserved; no security surface touched.
No operational impact: no version bump, binary identity unchanged, Cargo.lock regenerated and checked in, distribution channels unaffected.
Deferred to T03: init.rs provisioning split and library smoke test — correctly held back from T02.
Advisory (no action): cli/Cargo.toml serde derive feature technically unused in CLI (only trait bound at mcp.rs:329); harmless, matches plan.
